title: ServiceReferral
description: A request to connect an older adult to DFTA services — case management, home-delivered meals, benefits screening, caregiver support, elder-abuse help, transportation, or older adult center enrollment. NET-NEW: there is NO public API and NO Open Data twin for intake/referral; today an older adult, family member, or caseworker requests services only by calling Aging Connect (212-AGING-NYC / 212-244-6469), calling 311, or walking into a center. This schema serves both the read surface (list/get a referral's status) and the ServiceReferralRequest write surface (make a referral).

referralId.description: Server-assigned referral / case number. Omit on create; returned on the created resource.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Set by NYC Aging, not the requester.

serviceRequested.description: The kind of aging service being requested.

olderAdult.description: The older adult the referral is for.

ageRange.description: Coarse age band; DFTA services generally serve New Yorkers 60+.

preferredProviderDftaId.description: Optional DFTA ID of a specific provider/site the requester prefers.

urgent.description: Flag urgent situations (suspected elder abuse, no food, unsafe at home). Urgent referrals are routed immediately to Aging Connect / Adult Protective Services.

requestedBy.description: Who is making the referral (self, family, or a professional).
